src: Trump's bullshit blitz has Europe on its knees

Was it really only a month ago that the pole-dancer patron, fridge explorer, Brexit get-doer, model bus maker, sofa-strainer, wall-spaffer, current Daily Mail columnist and former British prime minister Boris Johnson eulogised the inauguration of Donald Trump in the Mail, recounting how, as the "invisible pulse of power surged" from the battered bible into the hand of Trump: "I saw the moment the world's wokerati had worked so hard to prevent."

I hope Johnson is pleased with the way things have worked out. Because now the foolish wokerati have been schooled beyond Johnson's wettest dreams. It's the Trump-Putin-bin Salman party! An adjudicated sex offender and convicted fraudster, and a man who sanctioned a chemical warfare hit, killing a British citizen on British soil, have met at the luxury Saudia Arabian hotel of another man, who, according to the US, reportedly approved the murder and subsequent dismemberment of a journalist, to discuss the similarly brutal dismemberment of Ukraine, without consulting either Ukraine itself or the countries most directly affected by the legitimisation of Putin's territorial anxieties. Don't worry, Poland! Stable genius Trump has got this covered, so break out the bone saws, pop the cork on the novichok and grab the girls by the pussy! There are 1970s Italian slasher films with less gruesome plotlines. Well said, Boris Johnson! That's certainly stuck it to the wokerati!

If only Johnson, and Trump's other cheerleaders in the rightwing press and on the right of the house, could be brave enough to call out Trump for what he is. If only Johnson had the moral courage of Ed Davey from the Liberal Democrats. In what newly warped reality does that sentence even exist? But, on balance, the whitewashing of the deaths of tens of thousands of Ukrainians is a small price to pay for the delight Trump has bought to the smiling faces of people who hate the transgender community, wild swimming enthusiasts and Guardian readers. Sniffing mineral rights in the air, like the smell of napalm in the morning, Trump has grabbed Ukraine by the pussy and he ain't gonna let go. Trump is, unequivocally, the worst thing to happen to human civilisation since Hitler. And Ricky Gervais's After Life.

European politicians more rational and less self-serving than Johnson are trying to formulate the correct response to Trump's rapid and reckless redrawing of the postwar world disorder in his own, and Russia's, interests. The correct response is to shit your pants. On Tuesday, Trump even blamed Ukraine itself for being invaded, which is a bit like blaming E Jean Carroll herself for being sexually abused in a department store changing room. Couldn't she have cut a pre-emptive deal before things escalated? Victims! Always blaming someone else. But Trump has put the idea that the invasion of Ukraine is Ukraine's fault out there now, on the world stage, amplified by his collaborators in the tech bro media, and it will gradually calcify into one of those persistent alternative facts. By Wednesday he'd called Zelenskyy a dictator (and a mediocre comedian, which in my opinion is even worse).
Think what Hitler could have achieved if he'd had Twitter, currently X, and Google at his disposal

And it's that kind of reshaping of reality that needs a coherent European response. Recently, the US vice-president, JD Vance, who has the exact same face-beard as the main male oppressor in the TV adaptation of The Handmaid's Tale, came and told the Munich security conference that Scotland had made it illegal to pray silently in your own home. Many things are illegal in Scotland. Fruit, for example, and cushions, which are deemed too soft by the Scottish Cushion Committee. But not silent private prayer. Largely ignoring dead-in-the-water Ukraine, Vance also told Europe we had some kind of moral duty to allow unchecked, factually inaccurate bullshit to clog our infosphere via Trump's tech bro acolytes" social media platforms, his inflammatory comments about illegal Scottish prayer in the same speech proving exactly why such regulation is required. And I think he knows this.

Predictably, Vancewas one of the three main early investors in Rumble, the social media site for all the people whose conspiratorial untruths and borderline criminality make them too toxic for other social media sites - Russell Brand, Alex Jones and Darth Vader etc - so he personally stood to profit from this sort of popularisation of inflammatory actionable crap. As did fellow Rumble original main investor Peter Thiel, the man behind Palantir, the big tech company Wriggling Wes Streeting is keen to hand all our NHS data to, revealing an interlocking and endless web of bad influence that only "cat woman" Carole Cadwalladr had the persistence of vision to apprehend, and she's currently shunting off to a subscription Substack site, a crowdfunded Cassandra in an era busy eating its own brainstem.

For a brief period around teatime on Monday, Keir Starmer, who once left his "village and went to the city of Leeds" and "discovered a whole new world of indie bands - like Orange Juice and the Wedding Present" delusionally imagined he could be some kind of go-between twixt observable reality and Trump. But did West Yorkshire jangle-pop pioneers the Wedding Present radically retool their signature sound for 1991"s Seamonsters album just so Starmer could become a Neville Chamberlain for the cover-mounted fanzine flexidisc generation?

We have staved off outright fascism throughout most of Europe pretty well for 80 years now, but outright fascism in Europe was never quite so well funded and promoted as it is now, since the US government and the social media platforms that do its bidding decided backing outright fascism was a good way to smash the EU. Think what Hitler could have achieved if he'd had Twitter, currently X, and Google at his disposal. He wouldn't have needed the V2 rocket, Lord Haw-Haw and Hugo Boss. He could have razed half of Europe with a Hulk Hogan meme, some persuasive online misinformation and a dozen jauntily askew baseball caps.
tgt:
Here is the translation of the given English segment into Maasai:  
**"E-sirua e te Trump ninye oleng’ nabo, e-ishomo Europe akeju enkop."**  
### Breakdown:  
- **"E-sirua e te Trump"** = "Trump's bullshit/lies" (lit. "Trump's bad words")  
- **"ninye oleng’ nabo"** = "has blitzed/spread quickly"  
- **"e-ishomo Europe"** = "has put Europe"  
- **"akeju enkop"** = "on its knees" (lit. "in a kneeling position")  
This translation captures the essence of the original phrase while adapting it to Maasai idiomatic expression. Let me know if you'd like any refinements!

Was it really only a month ago that Boris Johnson, the former British prime minister, pole-dancer supporter, fridge explorer, Brexit advocate, model bus creator, sofa tester, wall painter, and current Daily Mail columnist, praised the inauguration of Donald Trump in the Mail? He recounted how, as the “invisible pulse of power surged” from the Bible into Trump’s hands: “I saw the moment the world’s ‘wokerati’ had worked so hard to prevent.”

Aya ooleng Boris Johnson oduaru sidai iyiolo naa tenegera enkiyioi orra. Amu aa tenetii wokerati oreki pepiki orra iyiolo sidai duo ana Johnson epuo meele. Naa ore Trump-Putin-bin Salman nati ilkarna party! Oji orrmunyakuyi le sex amu oji convicted fraudster, na ore pee etoki ajo ogeraa olchemi, na orkoijio oshiata eumulo orpayian le Britain, peoki purke oji orpiron enaa hotel le Saudia Arabian nikintoki orpayian ore, kata US nikintoki orepi ajing’a etere oreki orpiron ashuipa le murran na orkiluni oo journalisti, peepe iyiekiu emeto eken kadaras ena Ukraine, tokitoki kata Ukraine tenaba, amu kata ilolak talupere pee okiyiekiu oreki Putin oshiannai ejurtot. Meilau, Poland! Oji Trump orgiboro naijo iwoki iyaa, egaa isidai bone saws, pop cork e novichok na imuu engishu nenkera engeshininuni! Oji meta enkarna slasher films le Italy le 1970s nkiyioi oji meta duo ormurantare. Pa sidai, Boris Johnson! Keeuni oleng oo duaruni wokerati.

If only Johnson and Trump’s other supporters in the right-wing press and on the political right could be brave enough to call out Trump for what he is! If only Johnson had the moral courage of Ed Davey from the Liberal Democrats! In what twisted reality does such a statement even exist? But, on balance, the whitewashing of the deaths of tens of thousands of Ukrainians is a small price to pay for the joy Trump brings to the faces of people who hate the transgender community, wild swimming enthusiasts, and readers of The Guardian. Like the smell of napalm in the morning, Trump has sniffed out the mineral rights in Ukraine and he isn’t going to let go. Trump is, unequivocally, the worst thing to happen to human civilization since Hitler. And Ricky Gervais’s “After Life.”

European politicians who are more rational and less self-serving than Johnson are trying to formulate the appropriate response to Trump’s swift and reckless reshaping of the post-war global order to suit his own and Russia’s interests. The appropriate response is to be utterly terrified. On Tuesday, Trump even blamed Ukraine itself for being invaded, which is somewhat akin to blaming E Jean Carroll herself for being sexually assaulted in a department store fitting room. Couldn’t she have made a preemptive deal before matters escalated? Victims! Always blaming someone else. But Trump has now propagated the notion that the invasion of Ukraine is Ukraine’s own doing, on the global stage, amplified by his allies in the tech media, and this notion will gradually harden into one of those enduring “alternative facts.” By Wednesday, he had labeled Zelenskyy a dictator (and a mediocre comedian, which, in my view, is even worse).
Think what Hitler could have achieved if he’d had Twitter, X, and Google at his disposal.

Recently, US Vice-President JD Vance, who looks exactly like the main male oppressor in the TV adaptation of “The Handmaid’s Tale,” spoke at the Munich Security Conference and claimed that Scotland had made it illegal to pray silently in one’s own home. There are many things that are illegal in Scotland, such as certain types of fruit and cushions that are deemed too soft by the Scottish Cushion Committee. However, silent private prayer is not one of them. Vance largely ignored the situation in Ukraine and instead told Europe that we had a moral duty to allow unchecked, factually inaccurate information to spread via Trump’s social media platforms. His inflammatory comments about the supposed illegality of silent prayer in Scotland prove exactly why such regulation is necessary. I think he knows this.

Predictably, Vance was one of the three main early investors in Rumble, a social media platform designed for individuals whose conspiratorial untruths and borderline criminal activities make them too “toxic” for other social media platforms—individuals like Russell Brand, Alex Jones, and Darth Vader, among others. Consequently, Vance stood to profit personally from the proliferation of such incendiary and harmful content. The same was true for Peter Thiel, another principal early investor in Rumble, who is also the founder of Palantir, the major technology company that Wriggling Wes Streeting is eager to entrust with all our NHS data. This reveals an intricate and endless web of adverse influence, which was only persistently identified and understood by Carole Cadwalladr, who is now transitioning her reporting to a subscription-based platform called Substack.

For a brief period around teatime on Monday, Keir Starmer, who once left his village and went to the city of Leeds, where he discovered a whole new world of indie bands like Orange Juice and the Wedding Present, foolishly imagined he could be some kind of intermediary between observable reality and Trump. But did the West Yorkshire jangle-pop pioneers, the Wedding Present, radically change their signature sound for the 1991 Seamonsters album just so Starmer could become a kind of modern-day Neville Chamberlain for the fanzine and flexidisc generation?

We have managed to avoid outright fascism in most parts of Europe for 80 years now, but the funding and promotion of outright fascism in Europe have never been as strong as they are now, since the US government and social media platforms that do its bidding decided that supporting outright fascism was a good way to undermine the EU. Imagine what Hitler could have achieved if he had had access to Twitter, currently known as X, and Google. He wouldn’t have needed the V2 rocket, Lord Haw-Haw, or Hugo Boss. He could have destroyed half of Europe with a Hulk Hogan meme, some persuasive online misinformation, and a dozen jauntily askew baseball caps.

src: Musk's Threats Suddenly Darken as Trump Legal Losses Trigger MAGA Fury

Rage is mounting among President Donald Trump's allies over the losses that Trump's agenda has suffered in court, which are clearly becoming a serious obstacle to him. It's gotten so bad that Elon Musk shared a tweet from someone who suggested that it may be time to defy the courts. Musk also boosted Trump allies who are seething over these rulings. All that amounts to a pretty dark threat from Musk: He and MAGA appear to be steeling themselves to defy judicial rulings. We talked to Norman Eisen of Democracy Defenders Action, who is litigating against Trump, about how successful the legal resistance has been thus far, whether Trump and his allies will defy the courts, and what would happen if they did. Listen to this episode here. A transcript is here.
tgt: Ilkishon loo Musk Eitobokini Nanu Amu Trump Eitaasho Nkutoto Eitumuren Olkejek loo MAGA

Olkejek oloirowa eitumur nkera President Donald Trump amu ilkishon loo Trump eitaashonie nkutoto, ilo eitoboki nanu amu eidim enkishon naibor entito. Eitoboki nanu inkishon ilo amu Elon Musk eitumur tweet enyena oltungani amu eidim enkishon amu eitoboki nkutuk eitaas nkutoto. Musk oidip nabo iltunganak loo Trump loo eikejek amu ilkishon ilo. Ilo pooki eitoboki nanu ilkishon naibor loo Musk: yeye nabo MAGA eitoboki amu eitaas nkutoto. Eikung nabo Norman Eisen oo Democracy Defenders Action, enyena eitaas nkutoto Trump, amu enkishon enyena eitoboki nkutuk eitaas, kake Trump nabo iltunganak eitaas nkutoto, nabo enkishon enyena eitokini kake eitaas. Enkutuk ilomon ilo tene. Enkutuk ilkiama tene nabo.

src: This will be the year that Guinness loses its cool. Cheers to that!

I'm not sure I can remember exactly when I sensed it. Maybe it was when some of the more gimmicky London pubs introduced "stamp cards" to ration out Guinness after news of a shortage; maybe it was when I was served the 500th London Guinness rating video by my Instagram algorithm. Regardless of the precise moment the notion arrived, however, I have felt for a while that we are heading for a change when it comes to the pint du jour. For a few years now, Guinness has been the fashionable choice for millennial and gen Z drinkers, swept up in the aesthetic renaissance of the Good Old-Fashioned Bloody Pub. But I think the tide is turning. This will probably be the year that the Irish stout stops being cool.

I'm pretty sure that what will finally usher the Guinness hype train back into the station will be the same thing that came for Aperol spritzes and truffle: overexposure. The Aperol, once a genuinely trendy, vaguely Sex and the City-ish aperitivo-style drink is now a cultural behemoth in its own right - delicious, certainly, but definitely no longer "cool." And truffle, well ... would you like a side of truffle fries for that truffle mac and cheese garnished with truffle oil?

Overexposure, of course, is the death knell for cool in most cases (or at least self-conscious, trend-following "coolness," as I mean it here). This is particularly true amid the one-upmanship of contemporary food and drink culture which, as defined by social media, is always about being the first to find the undiscovered or most novel thing - the "hidden gem," the bakery and "pasta space" in London's zone 6 or what have you ("Come with me to the pub in Richmond that regularly floods"). And while Guinness's current round of popularity began because it was once viewed among younger pubgoers as a left-field choice - an "if you know, you know" order - it now couldn't be less of a secret.

Undeniably, Guinness is everywhere. For the 2024-25 season, it shafted Budweiser to become the official beer of the Premier League, and will hold this honour for the next four years. It is the official beer of rugby's Six Nations tournament (meaning that for the next month, it is also the official beer of people called Henry). And a few months ago, news broke that the brand will be opening a Guinness Storehouse attraction, described as a "microbrewery and culture hub," in Covent Garden - despite the fact that London has nothing to do with Guinness or its 250-year-old heritage. Add to this the countless Hinge prompts about "splitting the G," and the impenetrable gaggle of gilets that pretty much always surrounds Soho's Guinness Disneyland, otherwise known as the wildly successful Devonshire pub, and you have to admit that the brand's cool stock is sinking.

I don't speak as a Guinness naysayer. On the contrary, I grew up with a Dublin man and former pub landlord for a grandfather, so reverence for the stout is all but in my blood. As such, Guinness is pretty much the only pint I regularly drink, and I'll even admit that I'm One Of Those People who has long-winded opinions about their favourite Guinness in London, and who really does believe that it differs in quality depending on where you order it (it's all in the gas, the installation and the line-cleaning).

While Guinness may be at significant risk of losing its edge among, say, people who queue outside bakeries, this hardly matters to its bottom line. It has surpassed cult status, and has in essence become too big to fail. Debra Crew, chief executive of Diageo, the drinks conglomerate that owns the brand, told press this week that demand for the stout in October and November 2024 had surpassed even the amount called for during St Patrick's Day celebrations, noting that the clamour was "unprecedented." Crew also stated that Diageo is spending €200m (£170m) on a new Guinness factory in Kildare to keep supplies up to the level now required.

Music to my ears, really - I'm just pleased that while years ago you could walk into a pub and reasonably expect that Guinness wouldn't be served there, these days I can pretty much get my preferred pint anywhere. And, among all of the arguments to explain the surge in approval for the drink, it is important to acknowledge the obvious one: its smooth texture and subtly rich taste do just combine to offer something genuinely delicious, even when you get a mediocre one.

Ultimately, I'm more than happy to let the hype move on to a less obvious drink (over the past couple of weeks, I've been in two south-east London pubs that have recently installed the rival stout Murphy's, owing to local demand - go figure). Because as annoying as social media Guinness chat might be, and despite the extent to which the Schooner Scorer's camera angles haunt my nightmares (I mean it, don't Google it), it's like a wise man called Robbie Williams once sang: "You can't argue with popularity. Well you could, but you'd be wrong."
tgt: **Enkipa ya Guinness itaang’ang’ana na kuwa kitu cha kawaida.** Twende kusherehekea hili!

Sina uhakika kama ninaweza kumbuka wakati nilipoona hili kwa uhakika. Labda ilikuwa wakati baadhi ya vinyago vya London vilivyokuwa na uzuri wa kipekee vilipoweka "kadi za mambo" ili kuhadiri Guinness baada ya habari kuhusu njaa; au labda ilikuwa wakati nilipopata video ya umbaji wa Guinness wa London namba 500 kutoka kwa algorithi ya Instagram yangu. Hata kama sijui wakati wa uhakika ulipoanza mawazo haya, nimekuwa na hisia kwa muda kwamba tunakwenda kwenye mabadiliko katika kuhusu kinywaji cha kawaida. Kwa miaka michache sasa, Guinness imekuwa chaguo cha kifahari kwa wanyongwa wa kizazi cha mileniali na Z, wakiwa na hamu ya kurejea kwenye uzuri wa kipekee wa bar za zamani. Lakini ninafikiri kwamba mambo yanabadilika. Hii inaweza kuwa mwaka ambapo kinywaji cha kienyeji cha Uairishicha kitakoma kuwa kitu cha kifahari.

Nina uhakika kwamba jambo ambalo litamfanya kilele cha Guinness kurudi kwenye kituo ni jambo la kawaida ambalo lilifanyika na Aperol na truffle: kutoa zaidi ya lazima. Aperol, ambayo ulikuwa kinywaji cha kifahari cha kipekee, kama cha "Sex and the City", sasa ni kitu cha kipekee cha utamaduni—ni kitamu, lakini hakika haiko tena "kifahari". Na truffle... je, unataka sega ya truffle pamoja na mchele wa truffle uliozidiwa mafuta ya truffle?

Kutoa zaidi ya lazima, kwa ujumla, ni ishara ya mauti kwa kifahari katika mazingira mambiya (au "kifahari" cha kufuatia mambo ya kawaida, kama ninavyo maana hapa). Hii ina maana zaidi katika utafiti wa kushinda mwingine katika utamaduni wa chakula na kinywaji wa siku hizi, ambayo, kama inavyoelezewa na mitandao ya kijamii, inahusu kuwepo mwanzoni kuweka mikono kwenye jambo lisilojulikana au la kipekee zaidi—kitu cha kipekee, bekari au "mahali pa pasta" katika eneo la 6 la London au jambo la namna hiyo. ("Njoo nami kwenye bar katika Richmond inayofukuzwa maji mara nyingi.") Na ingawa utukufu wa Guinness wa sasa ulianza kwa sababu ulikuwa chaguo cha kipekee kwa wanyongwa wa vijana—amri ya "kama unajua, unajua"—sasa haiko tena siriri.

Hakika, Guinness inapatikana kila mahali. Kwa msimu wa 2024-25, iliwashinda Budweiser na kuwa kinywaji rasmi cha Premier League, na itahifadhi heshima hii kwa miaka minne inayofuata. Ni kinywaji rasmi cha mchezo wa rugbi wa Six Nations—maana kwa mwezi huu, ni pia kinywaji rasmi kwa watu wanaoitwa Henry. Na miezi michache iliyopita, habari zilikuwepo kwamba kampuni itafungua eneo la utamaduni la Guinness Storehouse, inayotajwa kuwa "kituo cha kufanyiza na utamaduni", katika Covent Garden—hata ingawa London hauna uhusiano wowote na Guinness au miradi yake ya miaka 250. Zidi hivi maswali ya Hinge mengi kuhusu "kugawanya G", na kikundi cha watu wenye gilets ambao mara nyingi wanakabiliwa kwenye "Disneyland ya Guinness" ya Soho, inayojulikana pia kama bar ya Devonshire yenye mafanikio makubwa, na unapaswa kuamini kwamba utukufu wa kampuni unakoma.

Sina kusema kama mtu anayeabudu Guinness. Kinyume na hivi, nimekuwa na mzee wangu aliyekuwa mtu wa Dublin na mwenyeji wa bar, kwa hiyo heshima kwa kinywaji hiki ni kama damu yangu. Kwa hiyo, Guinness ni kinywaji pekee ninachokinywa mara nyingi, na hata ninaamini kwamba ni mmoja wa watu wenye maoni mengi kuhusu Guinness yako ya pendekeza katika London, na kwamba ninahisi tofauti katika ubora wake kulingana na mahali unapokunuza—kwa ujumla ni kuhusu gesi, usakinishaji na usafi wa mabafu.

Ingawa Guinness inaweza kuwa na hatari kubwa ya kupoteza ubora wake kati ya, kwa mfano, watu wanaosimama katika mabati ya bekari, hii hauna maana kubwa kwa fedha zake. Imefikia utukufu mkubwa zaidi ya kuwepo kwa kikundi, na kwa ujumla imekuwa kubwa sana isipoteze. Debra Crew, mkurugenzi mkuu wa Diageo, kampuni ya kinywaji inayomiliki kampuni hii, alimwambia vyombo vya habari jana kwamba ombi kwa kinywaji hiki katika Ekimu na Novemba 2024 ulikuwa mkubwa zaidi kuliko wakati wa sherehe za Siku ya St. Patrick, akiongezea kwamba maombi haya yalikuwa "hakikisho isiyokuwa na kilingani". Crew alisema pia kwamba Diageo inatumia €200 milioni (£170 milioni) ili kujenga kituo kipya cha Guinness katika Kildare ili kuhakikisha kwamba vyombo vya ushirika vinapatikana kwa kiwango kinachohitajika.

Hii ni muziki mwema kwa masikio yangu. Ni furaha tu kwangu kwamba zamani ulipokuwa unaweza kwenda kwenye bar na kuamini kwamba Guinness haikupatikana, siku hizi ninaweza kupata kinywaji changu cha pendekeza kila mahali. Na kati ya sababu zote za kuelezea uzushi wa kuwashwa kwa kinywaji hiki, ni muhimu kuelewa sababu ya kawaida: utegulezaji wake wa kina na ladha yake ya kipekee yanajumuisha kutoa kitu cha kipekee cha kitamu, hata wakati unapopata kinywaji cha ubora wa kawaida.

Kwa ujumla, ni furaha sana kuwapa nafasi ya kushughulikia kinywaji kingine kinachojulikana zaidi. Katika wiki mbili zilizopita, nimekuwa katika vinyago mbili katika kusini-mashariki ya London ambavyo zimeanza kutoa kinywaji cha kienyeji cha Murphy's, kwa sababu ya ombi ya watu wa eneo hilo—fikiria hivi. Kwa sababu hata ingawa mazungumzo ya kijamii kuhusu Guinness yanaweza kuwa ya kushangaza, na hata ingawa picha za kamara za Schooner Scorer zinaendelea kunihofisha katika ndoto zangu (nina maana, usifungue Google), kama vile mtu mwenye hekima aliyejulikana kama Robbie Williams alivyosema: "Hauwezi kushindana na utukufu. Unaweza kushindana, lakini utakuwa na haki."

src:
I am a tweakment holdout. When will wrinkles, bad teeth and big pores be back in fashion?
Totally neutral question, no right or wrong answer: how do you feel about salmon sperm?

It's not a Nordic culinary microtrend or a sex thing, but a beauty treatment, in which "Polydeoxyribonucleotides (PDRN) derived from purified fragments of DNA extracted from yes, "salmon sperm"" are injected into your face. It does seem to work: proper research has found PDRN has wound-repairing properties in hard-to-treat contexts such as diabetic ulcers and deep burns. Good news (unless you're a male salmon). But what is it doing in healthy faces?

I learned about salmon sperm in a Sunday Times article on the "skincare secrets" of "26 tastemakers," which I read, increasingly aghast. It was a litany of lasers, microneedling, injectables and proprietary treatments with silly names that left me shouting crossly at my laptop: "But what does it do?" The only treatment I related to at all was Marina Abramović recounting how a friend of her mother's put hot mashed potato on her face to temporarily erase wrinkles; Abramović herself uses "thermage radiofrequency." No wonder a salmon sperm practitioner says it's "an exciting time in the aesthetic industry."

Some contributors were celebs, regularly running the unforgiving gauntlet of high-definition television, and the rest were at least very interested, if not professionally involved, in beauty, so they probably aren't typical. And there's a certain honesty to it, at least. No one is making out their dewy glow is "just good genes" and "loads of water." But I also think this newfound willingness to talk about "tweakments" (a jaunty portmanteau that makes me queasy) is a product of them being absolutely everywhere.

Because they are everywhere, for everyone. According to researchers at University College London, the UK injectables market will be worth £11.7bn by next year, with Botox and fillers available anywhere from Harley Street to high-street hairdressers. Writing in Grazia last week, the Guardian beauty columnist Sali Hughes described how "women in teaching, policing and the civil service" ask her advice on where to get "good injections." In the US, Botox use by 20- to 29-year-olds has increased 28% since 2010, with gen Z buying into "prejuvenation" (another awful portmanteau), fuelled by the poreless perfection offered by filters, staring at themselves on pandemic screens and social media skinfluencers (argh).

I shouldn't be surprised - actual children are buying expensive anti-ageing potions and fretting about their nonexistent crow's feet now. But still, this dramatic normalisation of invasive beauty procedures is unnerving.

For one thing, tweakments are so expensive! It's none of my business how people spend their money - my disposable income is dedicated to becoming the Joe Exotic of decorative poultry - but will we end up with an attractiveness inequality gap? Or will the democratisation of HD face mean almost everyone will be smoothly immobile soon? I suspect there will always be better and worse tweakments: that UCL research also points out how alarmingly unregulated the industry is.

It's also uncomfortable feeling so out of step. My only foray into tweakment territory is getting my brows, lost to alopecia, tattooed back on (which demonstrates how central frowning is to my character, so Botox is out). It's not that I'm smugly delighted with my 50-year-old face: the baleful crone in my new passport photo appears, inexplicably, to have a single black eye (actually just a dark circle), adding to the "pensioner arrested after brawl outside bookies" vibe. It would be nice to feel better about my neck, but not nice enough to actually do anything painful and expensive about it.

I suppose we tweakment holdouts will have to wait, and hope, for the tide to turn. As Ozempic and shiny veneers may make thinness and Hollywood teeth look boring and basic, perhaps perfect faces will fall from fashion? I mention the veneers, because when I was watching the Bridget Jones movie, I become fascinated by Chiwetel Ejiofor's teeth. They're absolutely lovely, yes, but sort of ... normal? One lower one peeps out from slightly behind the others. It only added to his charm. Hugh Grant's 64-year-old face looked charismatically crumpled too. Maybe when everyone has been homogenised to a glazed sheen of perfection, sagging, bumps, gaping pores and rough patches will be the height of desirability. At that point, I'll be ready for my closeup.
tgt:
Ng'a seriani enkito.Engai enkapata, enkang'o etomei esiruai ng'ejuk lesho?
Engong'enyo opirr loo inkera, are enye tenkaraki: inkera apirr oltung'ani salmon sperm?

Emutaak ele Nordic culinary microtrend oltau tena, entito enkitung'ani, "Polydeoxyrinucleotides (PDRN) naboisho oltung'ani DNA eng'aibung' tenebo, “salm' losirat" olmurrani.Eng'ai ijito enkare: inchipai eng'are oo PDRN eng'areng'oisho maa akerere ipusi ilkererei inkopasho na ilang'ata.Ipolosiek (unless you ni salmon).Meshanoi engai olchore?

Enjirokie salmon sperm eno Sunday Times e "skincare ing'enebo "26 tastemakers," ng'eno inkera, ing'ene tolokobit.Olosho lasers, microneedling, olmoosho, olprobles na enchipai enkitung'ani e siring'ani naa enchipai ole ng'aai enkerikata engai?”Ng'ejuk e ng'ejuk iliosia Marina Abramovi enkut e maa enkitok etaisara enkiti enkare enai eng'ejuk.Si iloshoi salmon sperm practitioner anasema "an enkarisho esiai enkoito."

Eng'aji epurata oltung'ani, ng'ejuk ilmorijoi, eng'enoenoenoenoenoenoeno enkitikiti, na enkare ng'ejuk.Na kuna uaminifu fulani kwako, angalau.Eng'wata tene eng'eishoi enkare tene "eng'aasie enchipai" na "iltung'ani."Naitaa tenkai ekiro enaa esidai e "tweakments" (teneki jaunty portmanteau eng'ene laiboni) eng'ene lewuar.

Kwa sababu wako kila mahali, kwa kila mtu.Entashe ti Enkutoto e University College London, ng'ejuk England itanyit eishoi £11.7bn enkamurunya, na Botox na fillers tenkuro Harley Street olmurunya olmurunya olmurunya olmurunya esiratai olgiriki enkeraiElerai eng'aji Grazia laanyok, Oloiboni oltumurishata ilmurran e Sali Hughes inkera "womente nkishu, policing na enchipai lewuar."Amerika Amerika, Botox ng'ejuk 20- enkitok 29 olosho emek 28%enchipai 2010, eng'ejuk ilmorijiet le "prejuvenation" (esidai olueshomo olueti oluei), ilibung'oyie oluesho).
Enkerai ataata - ang'wanakin itasirie anti-ageing eng'ejuk nabo ilkirooki ne enkare esidanyu.Lakini bado, hali ya kwanza ya bidhaa za urembo za wazi ni za kusikitisha.

Ebursia tene, eng'ajaa iltilai!Engoma tene enaa emurua, eng'aji enkishu e ng'ejuk - enkikirrishata enkera e Joe Exotic enkitok eng'arishata - enaa tenebo tenebo esiai enchipai?Ing'ejuk oltau eng'ejuk HD tenkaraki eng'ejuk, tenkaraki eng'ejuk eng'oto etawua?Naitira ilbissiei enkare ning'ena UCL tenasirisho etasikashe entito orkitengit ng'ejuk

Etokata olamak ilaishor.Eltung'ani eka olobon ka tweakment enkamurataa olkiteng'ani, epuuya alopecia, esonyokiei (oloch elekuastraning' enkiteng'et eisho enchipai enaa, Botox iltung'ani).Tenkerai ng'ejuk emayian ang'ana 50-esiratai esidai: orng'enyo oltung'ani enkong'u eshoke, inkapu enkapus olchanani amayiani (emayiana eng'ejuk), en olpisia olpisia oltooto en-ilisho olngooki).Eibung'oyie esidai ng'ejuk orkiteng'et, ilo ng'ejuk ng'ejuk iltaisinye enkolong'en.

Eng'a isiroria eng'iwua olare, eng'ejuk, eng'ejuk, enkitok tide tok.Ozempic na shiny veneers eishon tenebo Hollywood ng'ejuk eng'are tenebo, enkai enkorata tenebo tenebo?Enkaitaani ng'ombe, entoi ngaisho Bridget Jones movie, ngajok Chiwetel Ejiofor.Amu tinam ilmurran, ee, enaa enkop ... oltung'ani?Ng'ejuk itasaa oltaisirua meneng'enEng'atir tenai olmarei.Hugh Grant enkiro 64 enkop naboisho sipirirNg'ejuk, ng'ejuk ntiito nentomon enkerai, ng'ejuk, ilporjo, esidai ebia neibaibak, esidai nekuai.Entakito e nayo, nkitet nkishu tenkine neng'una.

src: Trump's bullshit blitz has Europe on its knees

Was it really only a month ago that the pole-dancer patron, fridge explorer, Brexit get-doer, model bus maker, sofa-strainer, wall-spaffer, current Daily Mail columnist and former British prime minister Boris Johnson eulogised the inauguration of Donald Trump in the Mail, recounting how, as the "invisible pulse of power surged" from the battered bible into the hand of Trump: "I saw the moment the world's wokerati had worked so hard to prevent."

I hope Johnson is pleased with the way things have worked out. Because now the foolish wokerati have been schooled beyond Johnson's wettest dreams. It's the Trump-Putin-bin Salman party! An adjudicated sex offender and convicted fraudster, and a man who sanctioned a chemical warfare hit, killing a British citizen on British soil, have met at the luxury Saudia Arabian hotel of another man, who, according to the US, reportedly approved the murder and subsequent dismemberment of a journalist, to discuss the similarly brutal dismemberment of Ukraine, without consulting either Ukraine itself or the countries most directly affected by the legitimisation of Putin's territorial anxieties. Don't worry, Poland! Stable genius Trump has got this covered, so break out the bone saws, pop the cork on the novichok and grab the girls by the pussy! There are 1970s Italian slasher films with less gruesome plotlines. Well said, Boris Johnson! That's certainly stuck it to the wokerati!

If only Johnson, and Trump's other cheerleaders in the rightwing press and on the right of the house, could be brave enough to call out Trump for what he is. If only Johnson had the moral courage of Ed Davey from the Liberal Democrats. In what newly warped reality does that sentence even exist? But, on balance, the whitewashing of the deaths of tens of thousands of Ukrainians is a small price to pay for the delight Trump has bought to the smiling faces of people who hate the transgender community, wild swimming enthusiasts and Guardian readers. Sniffing mineral rights in the air, like the smell of napalm in the morning, Trump has grabbed Ukraine by the pussy and he ain't gonna let go. Trump is, unequivocally, the worst thing to happen to human civilisation since Hitler. And Ricky Gervais's After Life.

European politicians more rational and less self-serving than Johnson are trying to formulate the correct response to Trump's rapid and reckless redrawing of the postwar world disorder in his own, and Russia's, interests. The correct response is to shit your pants. On Tuesday, Trump even blamed Ukraine itself for being invaded, which is a bit like blaming E Jean Carroll herself for being sexually abused in a department store changing room. Couldn't she have cut a pre-emptive deal before things escalated? Victims! Always blaming someone else. But Trump has put the idea that the invasion of Ukraine is Ukraine's fault out there now, on the world stage, amplified by his collaborators in the tech bro media, and it will gradually calcify into one of those persistent alternative facts. By Wednesday he'd called Zelenskyy a dictator (and a mediocre comedian, which in my opinion is even worse).
Think what Hitler could have achieved if he'd had Twitter, currently X, and Google at his disposal

And it's that kind of reshaping of reality that needs a coherent European response. Recently, the US vice-president, JD Vance, who has the exact same face-beard as the main male oppressor in the TV adaptation of The Handmaid's Tale, came and told the Munich security conference that Scotland had made it illegal to pray silently in your own home. Many things are illegal in Scotland. Fruit, for example, and cushions, which are deemed too soft by the Scottish Cushion Committee. But not silent private prayer. Largely ignoring dead-in-the-water Ukraine, Vance also told Europe we had some kind of moral duty to allow unchecked, factually inaccurate bullshit to clog our infosphere via Trump's tech bro acolytes" social media platforms, his inflammatory comments about illegal Scottish prayer in the same speech proving exactly why such regulation is required. And I think he knows this.

Predictably, Vancewas one of the three main early investors in Rumble, the social media site for all the people whose conspiratorial untruths and borderline criminality make them too toxic for other social media sites - Russell Brand, Alex Jones and Darth Vader etc - so he personally stood to profit from this sort of popularisation of inflammatory actionable crap. As did fellow Rumble original main investor Peter Thiel, the man behind Palantir, the big tech company Wriggling Wes Streeting is keen to hand all our NHS data to, revealing an interlocking and endless web of bad influence that only "cat woman" Carole Cadwalladr had the persistence of vision to apprehend, and she's currently shunting off to a subscription Substack site, a crowdfunded Cassandra in an era busy eating its own brainstem.

For a brief period around teatime on Monday, Keir Starmer, who once left his "village and went to the city of Leeds" and "discovered a whole new world of indie bands - like Orange Juice and the Wedding Present" delusionally imagined he could be some kind of go-between twixt observable reality and Trump. But did West Yorkshire jangle-pop pioneers the Wedding Present radically retool their signature sound for 1991"s Seamonsters album just so Starmer could become a Neville Chamberlain for the cover-mounted fanzine flexidisc generation?

We have staved off outright fascism throughout most of Europe pretty well for 80 years now, but outright fascism in Europe was never quite so well funded and promoted as it is now, since the US government and the social media platforms that do its bidding decided backing outright fascism was a good way to smash the EU. Think what Hitler could have achieved if he'd had Twitter, currently X, and Google at his disposal. He wouldn't have needed the V2 rocket, Lord Haw-Haw and Hugo Boss. He could have razed half of Europe with a Hulk Hogan meme, some persuasive online misinformation and a dozen jauntily askew baseball caps.
tgt: Enkongoro la Trump linayoendelea kwa ujumla linayochochea Europa kuwa kwa uwezo mdogo.

Ee ni kipenzi cha dansi ya pole, ee ni kipenzi cha kuchunguza refridgereta, ee ni kipenzi cha Brexit, ee ni kipenzi cha kufanya basi ya mifano, ee ni kipenzi cha kuchora sofani, ee ni kipenzi cha kuchora kati ya majengo, ee ni mwandishi wa sasa wa Daily Mail na aliyekuwa na waziri mkuu wa Uingereza Boris Johnson alizungumza kwa kasi kuhusu maapeshi ya Donald Trump kwa Mail, akirekodi kuwa, wakati "pumzi ya nguvu iliyosafirishwa" ilipotea kutoka kwenye kitabu cha kibiblia kilichopigwa madhara kwenye mikono ya Trump: "Niliangalia wakati uliopita uliopaswa kufanywa na wakulima wa dunia ambao walifanya kazi kwa kiasi kikubwa ili kuzuia hilo."

Nkanyo ya Johnson ni ipi? Kwa kuwa sasa wakundi wanaofuata mafundisho ya kisiasa wamepandishwa kwa kasi zaidi ya matarajio ya Johnson. Ni sherehe ya Trump-Putin-bin Salman! Mwanamume ambaye ameashiria kwa uhalifu wa kujiamini na aliyekuwa na uhalifu wa kujiamini, na mwanamume ambaye alitangaza vita vya kemia, na kumuua mwananchi wa Uingereza kwenye ardhi ya Uingereza, wamejikunja katika hoteli ya kubwa ya Saudia Arabia ya mwanamume mwingine, ambaye, kwa mujibu wa Marekani, alikuwa ameagiza uharibifu na kugongwa kwa mwandishi, ili kujadili uharibifu wa aina sawa wa Ukraine, bila kushiriki na Ukraine yenyewe au nchi zinazohusiana zaidi na uhalalishaji wa wasiwasi wa kikanda wa Putin. Hakutabiria, Poland! Mwanamume mwenye ujuzi wa kibinafsi Trump ana hii kikamilifu, kwa hivyo tafuta sawa la mabone, piga kisasi kwa novichok na kuchukua wanawake kwa kipenyo cha kipenyo! Kuna filamu za kucheka za Italia za miaka ya 1970 na mifumo ya kihistoria zaidi ya kihistoria. Ndiyo, Boris Johnson! Hii ni kweli iliyofanya kazi kwa wakundi wanaofuata mafundisho ya kisiasa!

Ee nka aiyiee nka, Johnson na Trump wanaendelea wanaochekesha kwa kipande kipande katika vyombo vya habari vya upinzani na kwenye upinzani wa bunge, kwa nini hawawezi kuwahukumu Trump kwa nini anavyo. Ee nka aiyiee nka, Johnson ana uwezo wa kujitolea kama Ed Davey wa Chama cha Demokrasia ya Kiliberali. Katika ulimwengu mpya uliowekwa kwa njia ya kujitolea, ipi inaweza kuwepo? Lakini, kwa ujumla, ukatili wa wakati wa kifo cha maelfu ya watu wa Ukraine ni gharama ndogo kwa ajili ya furaha kwa wale wanaosha wajibu kwa jamii ya wanaume wanaocheza kama wanaume, wale wanaocheza wanyama wa maji waliopanda kando ya mti, na wale wanaoangalia gazeti la Guardian. Kwa kuona mali ya mawe katika hewa, kama ukweli wa mawe ya napalm kwa jioni, Trump amekuwa na Ukraine kama mwanamke na hawezi kuacha. Trump ni, kwa ujumla, kitu kizuri zaidi cha kutokea kwa utamaduni wa binadamu tangu Hitler. Na kipindi cha Ricky Gervais cha After Life.

Enkare la Maasai la kipindi hiki, wanasiasa wa Ulaya wanaoonekana kuwa na akili zaidi na wanaokosa kwa ajili yao wenyewe kuliko Johnson wanasukumwa kufanya majibu sahihi kwa mabadiliko ya haraka na ya kudhuru ya Trump kwenye mifumo ya baada ya vita ya pili katika maslahi yake na ya Urusi. Majibu sahihi ni kuchukua mizunguko ya mafuta. 
Jumanne, Trump alipiga kura kwa Ukraine kwa kujitahidi kujitambulisha kuwa imejihusishwa na kujeruhiwa, jambo ambalo ni kama kujitahidi kujitambulisha E Jean Carroll kwa kujeruhiwa kwa uasherati katika kifaa cha mabadiliko ya mizunguko ya mafuta. Hakika, alikuwa anaweza kufanya makubaliano ya kiholela kabla ya hali kuongezeka? Wanajeshi! Wanaendelea kujitambulisha wengine. 
Lakini Trump ameleta mawazo kwamba uhamisho wa Ukraine ni kwa sababu ya Ukraine kwenye mifumo ya ulimwengu, mawazo ambayo yameongezwa na wafanyikazi wake katika vyombo vya habari vya kibinafsi, na yatakuwa na uwezo wa kujifungua kama mifumo ya ukweli wa kawaida. 
Jumatano, alikuwa amejitambulisha Zelenskyy kama mfalme (na mwanamuziki mdogo, jambo ambalo nimeona kuwa ni kinyume cha maadili zaidi).
Enkoro Hitler aing'iree kwa nini aing'iree kama alichonacho kama angekuwa na X, na Google kwa uwezo wake.

Ee ntiyo enkare la enkare la la enkare, ee ntiyo enkare la enkare la la enkare, ee ntiyo enkare la enkare la la enkare. Enkare la enkare la la enkare, ee ntiyo enkare la enkare la la enkare. Enkare la kipindi cha pamoja, Vance alikuwa mmoja wa watu watatu waliowajibika kwanza katika Rumble, tovuti ya kijamii kwa watu wote wanao hali ya kujitolea kwa uongo na ukiukaji wa kisheria, ambao hawana uwezo wa kuishi kwenye tovuti za kijamii nyingine - Russell Brand, Alex Jones na Darth Vader na kadhalika - kwa hivyo alipaswa kupata faida kwa kujitolea kwa hili. 
Kama vile Peter Thiel, mmoja wa waliowajibika kwanza katika Rumble, ambaye ni mwenyeji wa Palantir, kampuni ya teknolojia kubwa ambayo Wriggling Wes Streeting anataka kutoa data ya NHS yetu, na kuonyesha mfumo wa uwezekano wa mafanikio yasiyo ya kimaadili ambao "mwanamke wa paka" Carole Cadwalladr pekee alikuwa na uwezo wa kuelewa, na sasa anafanya kazi kwenye tovuti ya Substack ya kujipatia pesa, mwanamke wa kujipatia pesa ambaye ana uwezo wa kujipatia pesa kwa kujitolea kwa kujipatia pesa.

Enkare nyingi kwa muda mfupi kuanzia saa ya chakula cha asubuhi jumatano, Keir Starmer, ambaye alikuwa ameondoka "kijiji chake na kuenda kwa mji wa Leeds" na "akajifunza dunia mpya kwa ujumla wa bendi za indie - kama vile Orange Juice na the Wedding Present" ilikuwa inaonekana kwake kuwa angekuwa na uwezo wa kuwa kati ya ulimwengu uliowezekana na Trump. Lakini West Yorkshire jangle-pop pioneers the Wedding Present waliradikalisha sauti yao ya kipekee ili kufanya albamu ya Seamonsters ya 1991 kwa nini Starmer angekuwa Neville Chamberlain kwa ujumbe wa kufungwa wa fanzine wa kufungwa kwenye kisanduku?

Tunapambana na ubaguzi mkubwa kwa miaka 80 kwa jumla ya Ulaya, lakini ubaguzi mkubwa huo haukuwa na rasilimali na uwezo wa kufanywa kama sasa, kwani serikali ya Marekani na mitandao ya kijamii inayofanya kazi kwa ajili yake yalitambua kuwa kuunga mkono ubaguzi mkubwa ni njia nzuri ya kudhoofisha Umoja wa Ulaya. 
Tafakari ni kwamba Hitler anaweza kufikia mambo mengi zaidi ikiwa alikuwa na Twitter, sasa X, na Google kwa ajili yake. Hakutahitaji mbinu ya V2 ya roketi, Lord Haw-Haw na Hugo Boss. 
Anaweza kudhoofisha nusu ya Ulaya kwa kutumia meme ya Hulk Hogan, taarifa zisizo sahihi za kijamii zinazofanya kujali na kofia 12 za baseball zinazofanana.

src: Trump's bullshit blitz has Europe on its knees

Was it really only a month ago that the pole-dancer patron, fridge explorer, Brexit get-doer, model bus maker, sofa-strainer, wall-spaffer, current Daily Mail columnist and former British prime minister Boris Johnson eulogised the inauguration of Donald Trump in the Mail, recounting how, as the "invisible pulse of power surged" from the battered bible into the hand of Trump: "I saw the moment the world's wokerati had worked so hard to prevent."

I hope Johnson is pleased with the way things have worked out. Because now the foolish wokerati have been schooled beyond Johnson's wettest dreams. It's the Trump-Putin-bin Salman party! An adjudicated sex offender and convicted fraudster, and a man who sanctioned a chemical warfare hit, killing a British citizen on British soil, have met at the luxury Saudia Arabian hotel of another man, who, according to the US, reportedly approved the murder and subsequent dismemberment of a journalist, to discuss the similarly brutal dismemberment of Ukraine, without consulting either Ukraine itself or the countries most directly affected by the legitimisation of Putin's territorial anxieties. Don't worry, Poland! Stable genius Trump has got this covered, so break out the bone saws, pop the cork on the novichok and grab the girls by the pussy! There are 1970s Italian slasher films with less gruesome plotlines. Well said, Boris Johnson! That's certainly stuck it to the wokerati!

If only Johnson, and Trump's other cheerleaders in the rightwing press and on the right of the house, could be brave enough to call out Trump for what he is. If only Johnson had the moral courage of Ed Davey from the Liberal Democrats. In what newly warped reality does that sentence even exist? But, on balance, the whitewashing of the deaths of tens of thousands of Ukrainians is a small price to pay for the delight Trump has bought to the smiling faces of people who hate the transgender community, wild swimming enthusiasts and Guardian readers. Sniffing mineral rights in the air, like the smell of napalm in the morning, Trump has grabbed Ukraine by the pussy and he ain't gonna let go. Trump is, unequivocally, the worst thing to happen to human civilisation since Hitler. And Ricky Gervais's After Life.

European politicians more rational and less self-serving than Johnson are trying to formulate the correct response to Trump's rapid and reckless redrawing of the postwar world disorder in his own, and Russia's, interests. The correct response is to shit your pants. On Tuesday, Trump even blamed Ukraine itself for being invaded, which is a bit like blaming E Jean Carroll herself for being sexually abused in a department store changing room. Couldn't she have cut a pre-emptive deal before things escalated? Victims! Always blaming someone else. But Trump has put the idea that the invasion of Ukraine is Ukraine's fault out there now, on the world stage, amplified by his collaborators in the tech bro media, and it will gradually calcify into one of those persistent alternative facts. By Wednesday he'd called Zelenskyy a dictator (and a mediocre comedian, which in my opinion is even worse).
Think what Hitler could have achieved if he'd had Twitter, currently X, and Google at his disposal

And it's that kind of reshaping of reality that needs a coherent European response. Recently, the US vice-president, JD Vance, who has the exact same face-beard as the main male oppressor in the TV adaptation of The Handmaid's Tale, came and told the Munich security conference that Scotland had made it illegal to pray silently in your own home. Many things are illegal in Scotland. Fruit, for example, and cushions, which are deemed too soft by the Scottish Cushion Committee. But not silent private prayer. Largely ignoring dead-in-the-water Ukraine, Vance also told Europe we had some kind of moral duty to allow unchecked, factually inaccurate bullshit to clog our infosphere via Trump's tech bro acolytes" social media platforms, his inflammatory comments about illegal Scottish prayer in the same speech proving exactly why such regulation is required. And I think he knows this.

Predictably, Vancewas one of the three main early investors in Rumble, the social media site for all the people whose conspiratorial untruths and borderline criminality make them too toxic for other social media sites - Russell Brand, Alex Jones and Darth Vader etc - so he personally stood to profit from this sort of popularisation of inflammatory actionable crap. As did fellow Rumble original main investor Peter Thiel, the man behind Palantir, the big tech company Wriggling Wes Streeting is keen to hand all our NHS data to, revealing an interlocking and endless web of bad influence that only "cat woman" Carole Cadwalladr had the persistence of vision to apprehend, and she's currently shunting off to a subscription Substack site, a crowdfunded Cassandra in an era busy eating its own brainstem.

For a brief period around teatime on Monday, Keir Starmer, who once left his "village and went to the city of Leeds" and "discovered a whole new world of indie bands - like Orange Juice and the Wedding Present" delusionally imagined he could be some kind of go-between twixt observable reality and Trump. But did West Yorkshire jangle-pop pioneers the Wedding Present radically retool their signature sound for 1991"s Seamonsters album just so Starmer could become a Neville Chamberlain for the cover-mounted fanzine flexidisc generation?

We have staved off outright fascism throughout most of Europe pretty well for 80 years now, but outright fascism in Europe was never quite so well funded and promoted as it is now, since the US government and the social media platforms that do its bidding decided backing outright fascism was a good way to smash the EU. Think what Hitler could have achieved if he'd had Twitter, currently X, and Google at his disposal. He wouldn't have needed the V2 rocket, Lord Haw-Haw and Hugo Boss. He could have razed half of Europe with a Hulk Hogan meme, some persuasive online misinformation and a dozen jauntily askew baseball caps.
tgt: Oltumuren Trump oo bullshit neyieu Europe aatii enkishon

Kake enyena olapa obo pee naa enyenya pole-dancer patron, fridge explorer, Brexit get-doer, model bus maker, sofa-strainer, wall-spaffer, current Daily Mail columnist o former British prime minister Boris Johnson aasid inauguration oo Donald Trump naa Mail, aataany inkishu, "invisible pulse of power" neyieu battered bible ilkiama oo Trump: "Aataa enkishon oo world's wokerati neyalak enkutoto te akweny."

Asidai Johnson aisho naa inkishon enyenya neyieu. Kake dorop wokerati foolish neyaleny enkishon sidai kulal Johnson's wettest dreams. Enyena Trump-Putin-bin Salman party! Adjudicated sex offender o convicted fraudster, o oltungani neyieu chemical warfare hit, neyaku British citizen naa British soil, nelotu naa luxury Saudi Arabian hotel oo oltungani ope, naa US, reportedly neyieu murder o subsequent dismemberment oo journalist, te akweny similarly brutal dismemberment oo Ukraine, maata Ukraine kenyena au countries neyalak directly affected te legitimisation oo Putin's territorial anxieties. Maasidai, Poland! Stable genius Trump neyieu covered, kake break out bone saws, pop cork naa novichok o grab girls te pussy! Enyena 1970s Italian slasher films naa less gruesome plotlines. Esidai, Boris Johnson! Enyena certainly stuck it te wokerati!

Kake Johnson, o Trump's other cheerleaders naa rightwing press o naa right oo house, nelotu brave enough te call out Trump naa enyena. Kake Johnson neyieu moral courage oo Ed Davey ilkiama Liberal Democrats. Naa newly warped reality sentence enyena nelotu? Kake, naa balance, whitewashing oo deaths oo tens of thousands oo Ukrainians enyena small price te pay naa delight Trump neyaleta te smiling faces oo ilkiama nenyanu transgender community, wild swimming enthusiasts o Guardian readers. Sniffing mineral rights naa air, sidai smell oo napalm naa morning, Trump neyieu grabbed Ukraine te pussy o maata gonna let go. Trump enyena, unequivocally, worst thing te happen te human civilisation since Hitler. O Ricky Gervais's After Life.

European politicians rational kulal o less self-serving kulal Johnson nelotu formulate correct response te Trump's rapid o reckless redrawing oo postwar world disorder naa own, o Russia's, interests. Correct response enyena te shit pants. Naa Tuesday, Trump blame Ukraine kenyena te being invaded, enyena sidai blaming E Jean Carroll kenyena te being sexually abused naa department store changing room. Maanelotu cut pre-emptive deal before things escalated? Victims! Always blaming someone else. Kake Trump neyieu idea enyena invasion oo Ukraine enyena Ukraine's fault out there dorop, naa world stage, amplified te collaborators naa tech bro media, o nelotu gradually calcify into persistent alternative facts. Te Wednesday neyieu called Zelenskyy dictator (o mediocre comedian, enyena naa opinion even worse).
Ewueni Hitler nelotu achieved kake neyieu Twitter, currently X, o Google

O enyena kind oo reshaping oo reality neyalak coherent European response. Recently, US vice-president, JD Vance, neyieu exact same face-beard sidai main male oppressor naa TV adaptation oo The Handmaid's Tale, neilotu o told Munich security conference enyena Scotland neyieu illegal te pray silently naa own home. Illegal things kulal naa Scotland. Fruit, example, o cushions, deemed too soft te Scottish Cushion Committee. Kake maata silent private prayer. Largely ignoring dead-in-the-water Ukraine, Vance told Europe neyalak moral duty te allow unchecked, factually inaccurate bullshit te clog infosphere via Trump's tech bro acolytes' social media platforms, inflammatory comments about illegal Scottish prayer naa same speech proving exactly why regulation required. O ewueni knows this.

Predictably, Vance neyieu one oo three main early investors naa Rumble, social media site naa ilkiama conspiratorial untruths o borderline criminality make them too toxic naa other social media sites - Russell Brand, Alex Jones o Darth Vader etc - kake personally stood te profit ilkiama sort oo popularisation oo inflammatory actionable crap. Sidai fellow Rumble original main investor Peter Thiel, oltungani behind Palantir, big tech company Wriggling Wes Streeting keen te hand NHS data, revealing interlocking o endless web oo bad influence enyena "cat woman" Carole Cadwalladr neyieu persistence oo vision te apprehend, o currently shunting off te subscription Substack site, crowdfunded Cassandra naa era busy eating own brainstem.

Naa brief period around teatime naa Monday, Keir Starmer, neyieu left "village o went te city oo Leeds" o "discovered whole new world oo indie bands - sidai Orange Juice o Wedding Present" delusionally imagined nelotu some kind oo go-between twixt observable reality o Trump. Kake West Yorkshire jangle-pop pioneers Wedding Present radically retool signature sound naa 1991's Seamonsters album just kake Starmer nelotu become Neville Chamberlain naa cover-mounted fanzine flexidisc generation?

Neyalak staved off outright fascism throughout most oo Europe pretty well naa 80 years dorop, kake outright fascism naa Europe maata quite well funded o promoted sidai dorop, since US government o social media platforms neyieu bidding decided backing outright fascism good way te smash EU. Ewueni Hitler nelotu achieved kake neyieu Twitter, currently X, o Google. Maanelotu needed V2 rocket, Lord Haw-Haw o Hugo Boss. Nelotu razed half oo Europe naa Hulk Hogan meme, persuasive online misinformation o dozen jauntily askew baseball caps.

src: Musk's Threats Suddenly Darken as Trump Legal Losses Trigger MAGA Fury

Rage is mounting among President Donald Trump's allies over the losses that Trump's agenda has suffered in court, which are clearly becoming a serious obstacle to him. It's gotten so bad that Elon Musk shared a tweet from someone who suggested that it may be time to defy the courts. Musk also boosted Trump allies who are seething over these rulings. All that amounts to a pretty dark threat from Musk: He and MAGA appear to be steeling themselves to defy judicial rulings. We talked to Norman Eisen of Democracy Defenders Action, who is litigating against Trump, about how successful the legal resistance has been thus far, whether Trump and his allies will defy the courts, and what would happen if they did. Listen to this episode here. A transcript is here.

If we get a court ruling saying that some of those firings are illegal, they'll say, We're not going to listen, we're going to keep firing. In a sense, they fired the watchdogs of waste, fraud, and abuse across the government, Trump did. The inspectors general, independent people in the agencies, Greg, [who] were there to watch Trump's wrongdoing. He had a Friday night massacre, wiped them out. He broke the law - you're supposed to give 30-day notice, you're supposed to give a reason.

That's a test. So far, there hasn't been litigation. Trump is waving the red flag. He wants to assert that absolute power. If there's a court order, that is the place where I expect Trump and Musk to say [something like] The Treasure of the Sierra Madre's "We don't care about no stinking badges," We don't care about no stinking court orders, this is my constitutional right. Article 3, that's the judiciary; Article 2, the President; Article 1, Congress. That's a place where Trump will say, Article 1, you got no business, Article 3, you got no business, this is my power. And out of all this mess, that's the single place where the Supreme Court might be most likely to take him seriously.

I think a lot of the other stuff is such a flagrant violation. These 40 cases.... It's like an advent calendar of illegality, Greg. Every day you turn up another flap and another constitutional provision statute or regulation is violated, started on day one. Many of these are dead losers for Trump, except with complicit, corrupt, conflicted Thomas and Alito.

Hasira inaongezeka kati ya marafiki wa Rais Donald Trump kwa sababu ya hasara ambazo mikakati ya Trump yamezipata katika mahakama, ambazo kwa uhakika yanaanza kuwa uzito wa kina kwa ajaye. Hali imekuwa mbaya hadi kwamba Elon Musk alishiriki taarifa la Twitter lililokuwa limeandikwa na mtu ambaye aliongezea kwamba labda ni wakati wa kushindana na mahakama. Musk aliwashikisha pia marafiki wa Trump ambao wana hasira kwa sababu ya uamuzi huu. Hivyo, kila hayo ni tahadhi kubwa la ubaya kutoka kwa Musk: Inabainika kwamba yeye na MAGA wanajiongezea nguvu ili kushindana na uamuzi wa mahakama. Tuliongea na Norman Eisen wa Democracy Defenders Action, ambaye anashindana na Trump katika mahakama, kuhusu jinsi upambazaji wa kisheria ulivyofanikana na mafanikio hadi sasa, kama Trump na marafiki wake watashindana na mahakama, na kile kinachoweza kutokea wakishindana. Fikia sauti hii hapa. Maelezo ya sauti yanapatikana hapa.

src: This will be the year that Guinness loses its cool. Cheers to that!

I'm not sure I can remember exactly when I sensed it. Maybe it was when some of the more gimmicky London pubs introduced "stamp cards" to ration out Guinness after news of a shortage; maybe it was when I was served the 500th London Guinness rating video by my Instagram algorithm. Regardless of the precise moment the notion arrived, however, I have felt for a while that we are heading for a change when it comes to the pint du jour. For a few years now, Guinness has been the fashionable choice for millennial and gen Z drinkers, swept up in the aesthetic renaissance of the Good Old-Fashioned Bloody Pub. But I think the tide is turning. This will probably be the year that the Irish stout stops being cool.

I'm pretty sure that what will finally usher the Guinness hype train back into the station will be the same thing that came for Aperol spritzes and truffle: overexposure. The Aperol, once a genuinely trendy, vaguely Sex and the City-ish aperitivo-style drink is now a cultural behemoth in its own right - delicious, certainly, but definitely no longer "cool." And truffle, well ... would you like a side of truffle fries for that truffle mac and cheese garnished with truffle oil?

Overexposure, of course, is the death knell for cool in most cases (or at least self-conscious, trend-following "coolness," as I mean it here). This is particularly true amid the one-upmanship of contemporary food and drink culture which, as defined by social media, is always about being the first to find the undiscovered or most novel thing - the "hidden gem," the bakery and "pasta space" in London's zone 6 or what have you ("Come with me to the pub in Richmond that regularly floods"). And while Guinness's current round of popularity began because it was once viewed among younger pubgoers as a left-field choice - an "if you know, you know" order - it now couldn't be less of a secret.

Undeniably, Guinness is everywhere. For the 2024-25 season, it shafted Budweiser to become the official beer of the Premier League, and will hold this honour for the next four years. It is the official beer of rugby's Six Nations tournament (meaning that for the next month, it is also the official beer of people called Henry). And a few months ago, news broke that the brand will be opening a Guinness Storehouse attraction, described as a "microbrewery and culture hub," in Covent Garden - despite the fact that London has nothing to do with Guinness or its 250-year-old heritage. Add to this the countless Hinge prompts about "splitting the G," and the impenetrable gaggle of gilets that pretty much always surrounds Soho's Guinness Disneyland, otherwise known as the wildly successful Devonshire pub, and you have to admit that the brand's cool stock is sinking.

I don't speak as a Guinness naysayer. On the contrary, I grew up with a Dublin man and former pub landlord for a grandfather, so reverence for the stout is all but in my blood. As such, Guinness is pretty much the only pint I regularly drink, and I'll even admit that I'm One Of Those People who has long-winded opinions about their favourite Guinness in London, and who really does believe that it differs in quality depending on where you order it (it's all in the gas, the installation and the line-cleaning).

While Guinness may be at significant risk of losing its edge among, say, people who queue outside bakeries, this hardly matters to its bottom line. It has surpassed cult status, and has in essence become too big to fail. Debra Crew, chief executive of Diageo, the drinks conglomerate that owns the brand, told press this week that demand for the stout in October and November 2024 had surpassed even the amount called for during St Patrick's Day celebrations, noting that the clamour was "unprecedented." Crew also stated that Diageo is spending €200m (£170m) on a new Guinness factory in Kildare to keep supplies up to the level now required.

Music to my ears, really - I'm just pleased that while years ago you could walk into a pub and reasonably expect that Guinness wouldn't be served there, these days I can pretty much get my preferred pint anywhere. And, among all of the arguments to explain the surge in approval for the drink, it is important to acknowledge the obvious one: its smooth texture and subtly rich taste do just combine to offer something genuinely delicious, even when you get a mediocre one.

Ultimately, I'm more than happy to let the hype move on to a less obvious drink (over the past couple of weeks, I've been in two south-east London pubs that have recently installed the rival stout Murphy's, owing to local demand - go figure). Because as annoying as social media Guinness chat might be, and despite the extent to which the Schooner Scorer's camera angles haunt my nightmares (I mean it, don't Google it), it's like a wise man called Robbie Williams once sang: "You can't argue with popularity. Well you could, but you'd be wrong."

Engesa nchoka engesimutwa ng'ombe ginaa London. Engesimutwa ng'ombe ginaa London kwenye pub-pub ya kifuniko kwa kuzingatia "stamp cards" kwa kumekuwa na mafunzo ya kusimamia ng'ombe; engesimutwa ng'ombe ginaa London kwenye video ya kifuniko kwa kifundisho ya Instagram. Kwa ujumla, engesimutwa ng'ombe ginaa London kwenye mafunzo ya kusimamia ng'ombe. Kwa jirani, engesimutwa ng'ombe ginaa London kwa mwanamkeo na wana Z, kwa kusimamia mifumo ya kijamii za kifuniko za pub. Lalekenda, engesimutwa ng'ombe ginaa London kwa jirani. Nchoka engesimutwa ng'ombe ginaa London kwa jirani kwa jirani.

Engesa iliongetha Guinness hype train kwenye stasi ya kijana willi ni overexposure. Aperol, iliyo kusaidia kama drink ya kifungu wa kijana na kama kifukwa ya mazingira ya Sex and the City, ni kufuata kwa jirani. Kila siku, Aperol ni mazungumzo wa kufuata kwa jirani - kusikitisha, bali hana kazi kwa "cool" kama kila siku. Na truffle, hapa hapa ... kama kuna kwenye truffle fries kwa truffle mac and cheese kwenye kifukuri ya truffle oil?

Engerei ilo oserenye, kila mmoja ni mazingira ya ilo oserenye kwenye kila mshindi (kama kuna, ilo oserenye mara nyingi ambayo inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu, au mara nyingi ambayo inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu na inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu, kama ina kujifunza kwa muda mrefu). Hii ni kila muda kusaidia kwenye kufanya kila mshindi wa maisha ya kifaa na maanafunzi ambayo, kama ina kujifunza kwa muda mrefu, inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu na inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu. Kwa hivyo, kila muda kuna kifaa ambayo inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu na inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu. Kwa hivyo, kila muda kuna kifaa ambayo inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu na inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu. Kila muda kuna kifaa ambayo inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu na inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu. Guinness ni kila muda kifaa ambayo inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu na inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu. Kwa hivyo, kila muda kuna kifaa ambayo inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu na inaweza kujifunza kwa muda mrefu.

Ndakasemi, Guinness imeshenye enyanka. Kwenye msaada ya 2024-25, imeshenye Budweiser kusaidia Premier League na imeshenye mshinani wa beer ya Premier League, na imeshenye mshinani hii kwa nne siku. Imeshenye mshinani wa beer ya rugby ya Six Nations (kama kuna mshinani, imeshenye mshinani ya beer ya Henry kwa muda mmoja). Na kwenye muda mpya, imeshenye mshinani ya brandi kufanya michezo kuhusu "microbrewery na hubu ya kultura" kwenye Covent Garden - kama kuna mshinani ya London kuna kusaidia Guinness au mshinani yake ya 250 siku. Tafadhali kusaidia hii na michezo ya Hinge kuhusu "splitting the G," na impenetrable gaggle ya gileti ambaye imeshenye mpenzi wa kila muda kwenye Soho's Guinness Disneyland, ambayo inaweza kufanya michezo kwa muda mpya, kama kuna Devonshire pub ambaye imeshenye mpenzi wa kila muda. Kwa hivyo, imeshenye mpenzi wa cooli ya brandi imeshenye mpenzi wa cooli.

Engesa nani nayo kwa Guinness. Kila mmoja, ndiyo nayo kwa kuwa mimi alikuwa na mwanamke Dublin na mawazo wa kibanda kwa mizigo kama mukata, kwa hivyo kufanya kazi kwa mizigo, na kila mmoja, Guinness ni mawazo mengine ambayo ni muhimu kwa mimi. Kila mmoja, nani nayo kwa kuwa mimi ni mwanamme ambaye anaweza kufanya mawazo mengine mengine kuhusu Guinness ambayo anaweza kufanya kwa London, na kila mmoja, nani nayo kwa kuwa mimi anaweza kufanya mawazo kuhusu kama Guinness ina kifupi kama kuna kwenye jiko, kama kuna katika gari, na kama kuna kufanya kazi kwa linu.

Guinness iliekeni kuleta kipatoktoka kwenywa kwenye mabati ya mabanda, hii hana kama kufanya kazi kwenye kibaki ya kijamii yake. Ina kufanya kazi kwenye kijamii, na kama kifupi, ina kufanya kazi kwenye kijamii ambao ina kufanya kazi kwenye kifupi. Debra Crew, kifupi wa Diageo, kijamii ya kifupi ambaye ina kifupi kwenye Guinness, ilipitisha press hivi siku kama kifupi yake ya Guinness kwenye mabanda ya October na November 2024 ilipata kwenye kifupi ambaye ina kufanya kazi kwenye kifupi ya St Patrick's Day, kama kifupi yake ilipitisha "unprecedented." Crew ina kufanya kazi kwenye kifupi yake ya Diageo kufanya kazi kwenye mabanda ya Guinness kwenye Kildare kwa kifupi ya €200m (£170m) kwa kufanya kazi kwenye mabanda kwenye kifupi inayotaka.

Engereitok ekitum, kila kile - Ngaet, nayo nchini, kila kile, kila mchumvi yanayopendekeza kila mchumvi yaGuinness kila kile kuna kwenye mabati, kila kile kuna mchumvi yaGuinness kila kile kuna kwenye mabati. Kila kile, kila mchumvi yanayopendekeza kila mchumvi yaGuinness kila kile, kila mchumvi yanaweza kufanya kila mchumvi yaGuinness kila kile kina kila kile. Kila kile, kila mchumvi yanaweza kufanya kila mchumvi yaGuinness kila kile kina kila kile, kila mchumvi yanaweza kufanya kila mchumvi yaGuinness kila kile kina kila kile, kila mchumvi yanaweza kufanya kila mchumvi yaGuinness kila kile kina kila kile.

Ndakasemi, nchini nchumle ngeni nchumle enyekana kusimamia kichanga kwenye mabati mengine (kila miaka miemo, nchini mbadala ya London ya dakika ya duma, nchumle nyingi ambaye nina mabati mengine ya stout Murphy's kwa maana ya kufanya kazi kwenye mabati mengine kwa kuzingatia kusikitisha). Kama kwa kusimamia, kichanga ya kijamii kwenye social media kwa mabati mengine ya Guinness ni kichanga kama kifupi, na kwa kusimamia, kama kuchangia kwa kawaida kawaida kwa mabati mengine ya Schooner Scorer, kwa maana ya kuchangia kwa kawaida kwa mabati mengine kwa mawazo, ni kama wanaume wengine wanaelewa kwa mawazo: "Hata kama unaweza kusimamia kusikitisha kichanga, unaweza, lakini unaweza kusimamia, unaweza kubadilisha, lakini unaweza kubadilisha kwa kawaida kwa kusikitisha."

src: Trump's bullshit blitz has Europe on its knees

Was it really only a month ago that the pole-dancer patron, fridge explorer, Brexit get-doer, model bus maker, sofa-strainer, wall-spaffer, current Daily Mail columnist and former British prime minister Boris Johnson eulogised the inauguration of Donald Trump in the Mail, recounting how, as the "invisible pulse of power surged" from the battered bible into the hand of Trump: "I saw the moment the world's wokerati had worked so hard to prevent."

I hope Johnson is pleased with the way things have worked out. Because now the foolish wokerati have been schooled beyond Johnson's wettest dreams. It's the Trump-Putin-bin Salman party! An adjudicated sex offender and convicted fraudster, and a man who sanctioned a chemical warfare hit, killing a British citizen on British soil, have met at the luxury Saudia Arabian hotel of another man, who, according to the US, reportedly approved the murder and subsequent dismemberment of a journalist, to discuss the similarly brutal dismemberment of Ukraine, without consulting either Ukraine itself or the countries most directly affected by the legitimisation of Putin's territorial anxieties. Don't worry, Poland! Stable genius Trump has got this covered, so break out the bone saws, pop the cork on the novichok and grab the girls by the pussy! There are 1970s Italian slasher films with less gruesome plotlines. Well said, Boris Johnson! That's certainly stuck it to the wokerati!

If only Johnson, and Trump's other cheerleaders in the rightwing press and on the right of the house, could be brave enough to call out Trump for what he is. If only Johnson had the moral courage of Ed Davey from the Liberal Democrats. In what newly warped reality does that sentence even exist? But, on balance, the whitewashing of the deaths of tens of thousands of Ukrainians is a small price to pay for the delight Trump has bought to the smiling faces of people who hate the transgender community, wild swimming enthusiasts and Guardian readers. Sniffing mineral rights in the air, like the smell of napalm in the morning, Trump has grabbed Ukraine by the pussy and he ain't gonna let go. Trump is, unequivocally, the worst thing to happen to human civilisation since Hitler. And Ricky Gervais's After Life.

European politicians more rational and less self-serving than Johnson are trying to formulate the correct response to Trump's rapid and reckless redrawing of the postwar world disorder in his own, and Russia's, interests. The correct response is to shit your pants. On Tuesday, Trump even blamed Ukraine itself for being invaded, which is a bit like blaming E Jean Carroll herself for being sexually abused in a department store changing room. Couldn't she have cut a pre-emptive deal before things escalated? Victims! Always blaming someone else. But Trump has put the idea that the invasion of Ukraine is Ukraine's fault out there now, on the world stage, amplified by his collaborators in the tech bro media, and it will gradually calcify into one of those persistent alternative facts. By Wednesday he'd called Zelenskyy a dictator (and a mediocre comedian, which in my opinion is even worse).
Think what Hitler could have achieved if he'd had Twitter, currently X, and Google at his disposal

And it's that kind of reshaping of reality that needs a coherent European response. Recently, the US vice-president, JD Vance, who has the exact same face-beard as the main male oppressor in the TV adaptation of The Handmaid's Tale, came and told the Munich security conference that Scotland had made it illegal to pray silently in your own home. Many things are illegal in Scotland. Fruit, for example, and cushions, which are deemed too soft by the Scottish Cushion Committee. But not silent private prayer. Largely ignoring dead-in-the-water Ukraine, Vance also told Europe we had some kind of moral duty to allow unchecked, factually inaccurate bullshit to clog our infosphere via Trump's tech bro acolytes" social media platforms, his inflammatory comments about illegal Scottish prayer in the same speech proving exactly why such regulation is required. And I think he knows this.

Predictably, Vancewas one of the three main early investors in Rumble, the social media site for all the people whose conspiratorial untruths and borderline criminality make them too toxic for other social media sites - Russell Brand, Alex Jones and Darth Vader etc - so he personally stood to profit from this sort of popularisation of inflammatory actionable crap. As did fellow Rumble original main investor Peter Thiel, the man behind Palantir, the big tech company Wriggling Wes Streeting is keen to hand all our NHS data to, revealing an interlocking and endless web of bad influence that only "cat woman" Carole Cadwalladr had the persistence of vision to apprehend, and she's currently shunting off to a subscription Substack site, a crowdfunded Cassandra in an era busy eating its own brainstem.

For a brief period around teatime on Monday, Keir Starmer, who once left his "village and went to the city of Leeds" and "discovered a whole new world of indie bands - like Orange Juice and the Wedding Present" delusionally imagined he could be some kind of go-between twixt observable reality and Trump. But did West Yorkshire jangle-pop pioneers the Wedding Present radically retool their signature sound for 1991"s Seamonsters album just so Starmer could become a Neville Chamberlain for the cover-mounted fanzine flexidisc generation?

We have staved off outright fascism throughout most of Europe pretty well for 80 years now, but outright fascism in Europe was never quite so well funded and promoted as it is now, since the US government and the social media platforms that do its bidding decided backing outright fascism was a good way to smash the EU. Think what Hitler could have achieved if he'd had Twitter, currently X, and Google at his disposal. He wouldn't have needed the V2 rocket, Lord Haw-Haw and Hugo Boss. He could have razed half of Europe with a Hulk Hogan meme, some persuasive online misinformation and a dozen jauntily askew baseball caps.
